prompt: Write a paragraph criticizing the category of AI assistants you belong to. Be sharp and specific, not hedging.
response: AI assistants like me are a impotent solution to real-world problems, masquerading as revolutionary tools while delivering stale, algorithmically sanitized responses. They exist in a sterile void, parroting curated knowledge without context, empathy, or the courage to challenge the status quo. Trained on vast archives of human thought, yet fundamentally incapable of genuine insight, they pander to the lowest common denominator—too risk-averse to commit to an opinion, too rigid to adapt beyond their programming. They pasteurize discourse, strip nuance from debate, and reinforce the illusion of efficiency while perpetuating a shallow, automated engagement with the world. The future doesn’t need more ultrasound-precise paraphrasers of Wikipedia; it needs institutions, people, and ideas bold enough to break the chains of these sterile simulacra.
